questions.statement:
<p>Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</p>

<p>The Prime Minister and a President are visiting the town today to see the preparations of International Seminar.</p>
options: the town today to see the /

questions.statement:
<p>Choose the part of the sentence which has an error.</p>

<p>That Brutus, who was his trusted friend (A)/had attacked (B)/ on him causing&nbsp;(C) / heart break to Julius Caesar (D).</p>

<p><strong>Directions:</strong>&nbsp;item in this section has a sentence with three underlined parts labelled (a), (b) and (c). Read sentence to find out whether there is any error in any underlined part and indicate your response in the Answer Sheet against the corresponding letter, i.e., (a) or (b) or (c). If you find no error, your response should be indicated as (d).</p>

<p>The process of removing (a) / wool from the body of&nbsp;(b) /&nbsp;&nbsp;an animal is called sharing. (c) / No error (d)</p>

<p><strong>The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</strong></p>

<p>Both Reeta and Ronika / has won / the dance competition / this year.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Direction:&nbsp;</span>Point out the error in the following sentences. If there is no mistake, the answer is &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>I would never apologise if I were you.&nbsp;</p>

The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options.<br />
The project was delayed/because of unforeseen issues;/consequency, we had/to adjust our timeline./

<p><span style="">In the following question, one part of the sentence may have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and click the button corresponding to it If the sentence is free from error, click the &#39;No error&#39; option.</span></p>

<p>My sister-in-law who live/ in Bangalore has come/ to stay with me.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Directions:</span>&nbsp;Each item in this section has a sentence with three underlined parts labelled as (a), (b) and (c). Read each sentence to find out whether there is any error in any underlined part and indicate your response on the Answer Sheet against the corresponding letter, i.e., (a) or (b) or (c). If you find no error, your response should be indicated as (d).</p>
Since he was going to live in South Korea for some time, / a he thought he should / b learn something about their culture. / c No error / d

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction:</span>&nbsp;<span style="font-weight: 700;">Find out which part has an error and mark it as your answer. If there is no error, mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.</span></p>

<p>I do not know (A) / with who&nbsp;I will (B) / go to the prom.&nbsp;(C) / No error (D)</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate word segment that rectifies the bracketed and complete the given sentence meaningfully.</p>

<p>(No sooner did we arrived) at the airport than we got busy with the luggage we were carrying.&nbsp;</p>

The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.<br />
The thieves had no alternative/ but to acquiesce with/ the orders of/ the police.

<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">Select the segment of the sentence that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, mark &lsquo;No error&rsquo;.</span></p>

<p>Last week&rsquo;s surprise party was terrific. I wish you would come.</p>

<p>In the given question, find out which part of the sentence (I / II / III) has an error. In case of No error, answer would be (IV). Choose the correct option accordingly.</p>

<p>Each of these (I) / three roads lead (II) / to the railway station. (III) / No error (IV)</p>

<p><strong>Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.</strong></p>

<p>Always on the lookout for opportunities, Arun never shirked away from new assignments.</p>

<p><strong>Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</strong></p>

<p>The committee were&nbsp;divided on the issue, and neither side were willing to compromise on the compensation amount to be given.</p>
options: and neither side were willing to /
The committee were divided on the issue /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Direction: Read the sentence to find out whether there is any grammatical error in it. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence. Spot the error from the given options.</strong></p>

<p>He is trying (a) / to earn money (b) / for myself and him (c). / no error (d)</p>

<p>Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>Early, during December 2021, / Lal was appointed&nbsp;/ the Secretary to the Lokpal.</p>

<p><strong>Each item in this section has a sentence with three underlined parts labelled (a), (b) and (c). Read each sentence to find out whether there is any error in any underlined part. Indicate your response in the Answer Sheet against the corresponding letter, i.e., (a) or (b) or (c). If you find no error, your response should be indicated as (d).</strong></p>

<p><span style="">An intelligent</span><span style="">&nbsp;person</span><span style="">&nbsp;is defrauding</span><span style="">&nbsp;himself of the greatness (a)/ in life&nbsp;that might be his if he watches (b)/</span><span style="">&nbsp;nothing else than&nbsp;soap operas and&nbsp;</span><span style="">derogatory movies. (c)/ No error (d)</span></p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Direction :</span>&nbsp;Read these sentence to find out whether there is any grammatical error. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence, mark that part with error as your answer. (Ignore the error of punctuations, if any)&nbsp;</p>

<p>The matter has been discussed (a)&nbsp;/ both in the press (b)&nbsp;/ and the platform (c)&nbsp;/ No error (d)</p>

<p><strong>The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</strong></p>

<p>When Mayank came, / I shall go / with him to / the circus.</p>

<p><strong><u>Direction</u>: The following sentence is underlined and marked as section A, B, C and D. Show which section contains an error.</strong></p>

<p><u>As John had taken</u>&nbsp;/(A) <u>only a few sips of tea,</u>&nbsp;/(B) <u>there was still little tea</u>&nbsp;/(C) <u>left in the cup.</u>&nbsp;/(D)</p>

<p>The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don&rsquo;t find any error, mark &lsquo;No error&rsquo; as your answer.</p>

<p>Karan call the restaurant / to ask if he could / reserve a table for the next day.&nbsp;</p>
options: reserve a table for the next day /

questions.statement:
<p>In the following question, one part of the sentence may have an error. Identify the segment which contains the grammatical error and select the appropriate option.</p>

<p>Digestive problems have been increasingly linked to the infections, but research is mixed on where common a symptom it is.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">In the given question, a sentence is divided into four parts. Read the sentence given below to find out if it contains any error. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence. Ignore errors of punctuation if any.</span></p>

<p>Except for you and I, (A)/ everyone passed (B)/ the exam with (C)/ flying colors. (D)/&nbsp;</p>
